stereotype: «ResourceAccess» — owns revenueLedger (append-only financial-class Postgres log of inbound revenue events and reversals)
volatility: Revenue Ledger STORAGE volatility — how revenue-share financial facts (inbound end-user payments, reversals/chargebacks) are persisted and read back. The persistence mechanism (Postgres/CNPG today; a different financial store or managed-ledger service tomorrow) is infrastructure-opaque to callers.

ops.stereotype: pure write — append immutable inbound revenue fact
ops.note: Idempotent append of an inbound payment fact; duplicate gateway event id collapses to existing entry (no double-credit).

fields.name: Entry
fields.note: Immutable inbound revenue fact; Entry.GatewayEventID is the dedup token (UNIQUE constraint)

fields.name: EntryRef
fields.note: Opaque append-position reference; on duplicate returns the prior entry's ref

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — Transient (retryable) | Infrastructure (retryable) | ContractMisuse (non-retryable); duplicate gateway event id is success, not error

ops.stereotype: pure write — append immutable reversal fact
ops.note: Idempotent append of a chargeback/reversal fact as a new row; never mutates the original entry (forward-only compensation).

fields.name: Reversal
fields.note: Reversal/chargeback fact; Reversal.GatewayEventID is the dedup token; ReversesGatewayEventID optional back-link

fields.name: EntryRef
fields.note: Opaque append-position reference; on duplicate returns the prior reversal's ref

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse; duplicate gateway event id is success

ops.note: Replays all immutable inbound and reversal entries for a billing cycle in append order; empty cycle returns empty slice.

fields.name: Entries
fields.note: All inbound and reversal entries for the cycle in append order; empty slice when no facts recorded

fields.name: (error)
fields.note: Method layer-typed error — Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse; empty cycle is empty slice, not NotFound

dataContracts: GatewayEventID = string // merchant gateway globally-unique event id (dedup token)
Money = { MinorUnits int64; Currency string } // signed minor-unit integer + ISO-4217; never float
EntryRef = string // opaque append-position reference returned by write verbs
RevenueShareSplit = { PlatformFee Money; MerchantPayout Money; ComputeCostRecover Money } // optional at ingest
RevenueEntry = { CustomerID; CycleID; Kind RevenueKind; Amount Money; GatewayEventID; Split *RevenueShareSplit; OccurredAt time.Time; RecordedAt time.Time; Ref EntryRef }
ReversalEntry = { CustomerID; CycleID; Amount Money; GatewayEventID; ReversesGatewayEventID *GatewayEventID; OccurredAt time.Time }
fwra.Error = { Kind: Transient | Infrastructure | ContractMisuse } // no Conflict kind (append-only); duplicate event id is success
errorModel: framework-go/resourceaccess.Error — kinds: Transient (retryable), Infrastructure (retryable), ContractMisuse (non-retryable). No Conflict kind — append-only ledger has no optimistic-concurrency contention. No public Duplicate error — duplicate gateway event id is mapped to idempotent success (prior EntryRef returned). Retryable seeded on construction, never inferred at call site.
idempotency: Write verbs: caller-supplied gateway event id + DB UNIQUE constraint. A duplicate event id returns the prior EntryRef as success (no second append, no error). Read: pure, trivially idempotent. No fwra.IdempotencyKey reference — the domain gateway event id is the natural dedup token for webhook-delivered financial facts.

revisions.summary: Initial contract — three ops (recordInboundRevenue, recordReversal, readRange). Append-only ledger discipline justified against operational-concepts §13. 9/9 PASS App C §6.

APPROVED-AND-FROZEN. Append-only discipline, no-Conflict, duplicate→success, gateway-event-id-as-domain-param all confirmed. OQ-1..OQ-6 adjudicated; OQ-3 (aggregate key CustomerID vs SettlementID) NOT SETTLED — escalated to D-MST as a hard cross-component dependency. One editorial amendment: 'frozen usageAccess sibling' corrected to 'planned/§13-classified'.
